reviewer: retrospective critique backfill (code-grounded + existing conformance suite), revision 1

rubric.evidence: worker.js loads a REAL transformers.js translation pipeline (pipeline('translation', 'Xenova/nllb-200-distilled-600M', {device:'wasm', dtype:'q8'})) and calls pipe(text, {src_lang, tgt_lang, num_beams, max_new_tokens:256}) on-device — ONE model translating directly between any of 200 FLORES-200 languages (many low-resource). Streaming via TextStreamer for greedy decode. Conformance calls-real-inference (source) + loads-clean (console-clean) tested pass; io-shape-semantic manual-evidenced. No measured latency fabricated.
rubric.notes: The See-inside surface exposes the REAL mechanism: NLLB steers output language by forcing the decoder to start with the target language's BOS token (forced_bos_token_id). forcedBosId() reads that exact token id back out of the tokenizer (trying several version-dependent lookup shapes) and the page reports it — a genuine 'here's how it picks the language' view, not a caption.

rubric.evidence: Expected output = translation_text. worker.js returns {translation, srcCode, tgtCode, forcedBos, inTokens, outTokens, beams, ms} — the real forced-BOS token id, input_ids length, and streamed output. io-shape-semantic conformance assertion covers a real translation in the chosen target language.
rubric.notes: forcedBos may be null if the tokenizer lookup shape is unavailable in the loaded transformers.js version — handled gracefully (the translation still runs; only the See-inside id shows unavailable).

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares Xenova/nllb-200-distilled-600M (linked, 5 occurrences), the translation task, q8 dtype, WASM backend, ~900 MB and CC-BY-NC-4.0 license — matching models.json. declares-model-id/task/backend/quantisation conformance assertions tested pass.
rubric.notes: CC-BY-NC-4.0 is NON-COMMERCIAL — correctly recorded in at-a-glance (and models.json). ~900 MB is the LARGEST download in this batch. Inference off the main thread.

rubric.evidence: The page uses the shared createModelLoader auto-init (modelId Xenova/nllb-200-distilled-600M, q8, ~900 MB, requiresWebGPU:false); a valid current cached copy auto-initialises with no Load click. shared-auto-init-loader conformance assertion tested pass.
rubric.notes: At ~900 MB the never-silently-re-download guarantee, integrity check before ready, and Clear-cache control are the most consequential in the batch. Size prominently surfaced in at-a-glance.

rubric.evidence: runStatus role=status aria-live; streamed partial translation in an aria-live region; worker errors surface on the page; shared loader adds checking→initialising→ready + Retry. live-status-region + errors-on-the-page conformance assertions tested pass; honest-progress-retry-offline / runs-without-webgpu-fallback manual-evidenced.
rubric.notes: WASM-only. On a memory-constrained mobile device ~900 MB may be evicted or fail to load; the shared loader's Re-download / honest-failure path handles that without faking success.

rubric.evidence: Real, labelled controls: the source text <textarea>, source/target FLORES-200 code pickers, a Beams range slider, and a translate button. All keyboard-operable. controls-operable conformance assertion is manual-evidenced.
rubric.notes: The FLORES-200 code pickers map to the real src_lang/tgt_lang args and the forced-BOS mechanism the See-inside surface exposes.

rubric.evidence: No canned translation: streamed text, token counts, and the forced-BOS token id are all read from the real pipeline/tokenizer. no-fake-output conformance assertion is manual-evidenced.
translate button + language pickers carry names (buttons-have-names tested pass); status role=status aria-live; output region aria-live; page lang=en; WCAG-AA light+dark. images-have-alt tested pass.
rubric.notes: As with m2m100, the translated output region uses page lang=en rather than a dynamic target-language lang — an open item for correct screen-reader pronunciation.

rubric.evidence: responsive-desktop/responsive-mobile (no-horizontal-overflow) conformance assertions present; design-system panels + flex-wrap pickers. models.json support is needs-review on both classes (automated pre-scan flagged sub-24px tap targets), pending an agent matrix pass to confirm picker/slider tap sizes and flip to ok.
Translation runs in the Web Worker; tokens stream to the main thread which only appends text. inference-off-main-thread conformance assertion tested pass; inp-stays-low manual-evidenced.
rubric.notes: The 600M model is heavy — decode is slower than M2M100/Marian — but off-thread + streaming, so it does not block INP; the cost is perceived latency, honestly reported via the streamed output and ms.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low move heavy work off main thread web worker
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Run heavy generation off the main thread and stream progress; keep INP low.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — translation runs in the worker; TextStreamer streams the greedy decode so the UI stays responsive on the 600M model.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js off-thread streaming; inference-off-main-thread conformance assertion tested pass

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible form labels select menu text input
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Label every control including language pickers; keyboard-operable.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — source textarea, FLORES-200 source/target pickers and the beams slider are labelled and keyboard-operable.

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status role status aria-live
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce state via role=status aria-live; consider a lang attribute on translated output.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: partially applied — status is role=status aria-live and output is aria-live; a dynamic output lang attribute matching the target language is a recorded follow-up.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: live-status-region + buttons-have-names tested pass; dynamic output lang open item

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel without horizontal overflow flex grid
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Fluid flex/grid with wrapping; avoid fixed widths.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — design-system panels; the FLORES-200 pickers flex-wrap on narrow viewports.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: responsive assertions present; support needs-review pending agent matrix pass

openQuestions: ~900 MB is the largest auto-init download in the batch — should the first load carry a stronger explicit confirmation / metered-connection warning on mobile, given eviction risk on constrained devices?
Should the translated output region get a dynamic lang attribute matching the FLORES-200 target for correct screen-reader pronunciation?

followUpGoals.goal: Add a more prominent size/metered-connection notice before the first ~900 MB load on mobile, and set a dynamic lang attribute on the translated output region.
Run the mobile+desktop responsive matrix, confirm/raise picker and slider tap targets, and flip models.json support to ok.

summary: Retrospective, code-grounded revision-1 critique for nllb-200-translation (Xenova/nllb-200-distilled-600M, translation, q8, ~900 MB, CC-BY-NC-4.0). Real 200-language translation: worker.js runs the transformers.js pipeline off the main thread with src/tgt FLORES-200 codes, streams tokens via TextStreamer, and — notably — exposes the REAL forced_bos_token_id language-steering mechanism by reading the exact token id back out of the tokenizer for the See-inside surface. Auto-init via createModelLoader; role=status aria-live states; no fake output. The non-commercial CC-BY-NC-4.0 license and the ~900 MB size (largest in the batch) are correctly recorded in at-a-glance + models.json. Verified against the immutable conformance suite (calls-real-inference, loads-clean, declares-*, inference-off-main-thread, shared-auto-init-loader, live-status-region, buttons-have-names tested pass; io-shape/parity/inp manual-evidenced). No fabricated numbers. Honest weaknesses: ~900 MB is heavy and eviction-prone on mobile (needs a stronger pre-load notice); output region lacks a dynamic target-language lang; models.json support needs-review pending a tap-target matrix pass.
